title: Narrow Console Table (Super Simple Collection)
summary: A narrow console on three identical 2x4 H-frames spaced along its length, with a two-board 1x8 shelf resting on the middle rail and a single 1x12 top capping the whole thing.
description:
Build three identical H-shaped 2x4 frames — two legs each, joined by a top, middle, and bottom cross brace — spaced out along the console's length: one at each end and one in the middle for support on a run this long. Getting all three frames genuinely identical is what keeps the shelf and top sitting flat once everything is assembled.

The two 1x8 shelf boards rest across the middle cross brace of all three frames, forming a simple open shelf without any additional framing — this is the entire point of the "Super Simple Collection": no aprons, no face frame, no joinery beyond straight butt joints and screws.

A single 1x12 board caps the whole assembly as the top, screwed down into the top cross brace of each of the three frames.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the three H-frames
steps.body: Build three identical H-shaped frames, each from two legs and a top, middle, and bottom cross brace.

steps.title: Position the frames
steps.body: Stand the three frames upright, spaced evenly along the console's length — one at each end and one in the middle.

steps.title: Add the shelf
steps.body: Attach the two shelf boards across the middle cross brace of all three frames.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Screw the single top board down into the top cross brace of each frame.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Narrow console table on three simple 2x4 H-frames
